description: A truth and a choice make a choice of truths.
goal: (-> (and A (or B C)) (or (and A B) (and A C)))

hints.text: This is the **distributive law**: $A \land (B \lor C)$ is equivalent to $(A \land B) \lor (A \land C)$. You have $A$ and a choice between $B$ and $C$ — either way, $A$ comes with. Start by assuming $A \land (B \lor C)$.
Peel off $B \lor C$ from the right.
Now [proof-by-cases|proof by cases] on the [disjunction|disjunction]. First, assume $B$.
Slot $A \land B$ on the left of the target [disjunction|disjunction] with `or-intro-l`, $A \land C$ on the right.
Slot $A \land C$ on the right with `or-intro-r`, $A \land B$ on the left.
Both cases led to $(A \land B) \lor (A \land C)$. Use `or-elim` on the $B \lor C$ from step 3.
[assumption|Discharge] the [conjunction|conjunction] to finish. Truth distributed, frosting chosen.
